heroCompanyDescription: Ora Banda Mining Limited (ASX: OBM) is an Australian gold exploration and development company focused on its project portfolio in the Ora Banda district of Western Australia, a historically productive gold region within the broader Eastern Goldfields. The company's flagship asset is the Round Dam deposit, which was upgraded tenfold to 1.33 million ounces in March 2026, transforming OBM into a company with a resource base of genuine development scale.

skew.rationale: The probability distribution is skewed modestly to the upside, driven by the combination of a transformational resource upgrade, an active exploration pipeline, and a record gold price environment that materially improves project economics. The primary constraint on a more aggressive bullish skew is the absence of financial disclosure, which prevents assessment of balance sheet capacity to fund development and introduces meaningful dilution risk.

verdict.text: At A$1.13 — up 31.9% on the session and near the 52-week high of A$1.67 — OBM is priced as a credible but unfunded gold developer at an early but accelerating stage of a resource re-rating cycle. The market has rapidly absorbed the tenfold Round Dam resource upgrade, the Little Gem and Sapphire Trend exploration success, and the formalised OSA, but has not yet assigned a funded development premium. With gold near US$4,990/oz, the in-situ value of the 1.33 million ounce resource is substantial, but the stock continues to trade at a steep discount reflecting the absence of a scoping study, unresolved capital requirements, and a multi-year production timeline. The next material inflection point is the March 2026 quarterly report in late April, which will either validate the OSA cash flow thesis or expose the capital constraint risk that underpins the bear case.

featuredRationale: The hypothesis distribution assigns 40% to the base case of continued exploration progress with an extended development timeline, 30% to a full resource re-rating bull case, 22% to a bear case driven by capital constraints or gold price reversal, and 8% to a tail catalyst scenario involving M&A or institutional discovery. The net skew is modestly bullish, with upside scenarios (N1 + N4) totalling 38% against a 22% bear case, but the base case dominates given the significant information gaps around OBM's financial position.

identity.overview: Ora Banda Mining Limited (ASX: OBM) — auto-added to coverage. Full company overview pending analyst research.

hypotheses.title: N1: Growth/Recovery
hypotheses.description: The tenfold increase in Round Dam Mineral Resource to 1.33 million ounces, combined with the Little Gem expansion, new Sapphire Trend discovery, and the formalised OSA for third-party processing, catalyses a sustained re-rating of OBM as a credible mid-tier gold developer. With gold near US$4,990/oz, the in-situ value of the resource base is substantial, and institutional engagement evidenced by the March 2026 site visit supports a path toward a funded development decision. This scenario requires a scoping study or PFS announcement and capital markets execution to fully materialise.
supporting: Round Dam Mineral Resource grew tenfold to 1.33 million ounces (ASX, 2026-03-10), a transformational upgrade for a company of OBM's size
Gold price at US$5,027.9/oz (+3.0% on the day of analysis), providing exceptional margin leverage for any ounces brought into production
New Sapphire Trend discovery and Little Gem expansion (ASX, 2026-03-11) suggest the exploration pipeline remains active and open-ended
Formalisation of third-party processing OSA (ASX, 2026-03-13) reduces near-term capex burden and accelerates potential production timelines
OBM's 52-week range of A$0.64 to A$1.67 shows the market has already begun re-rating the stock from deeply depressed levels
contradicting: No financial statements or production data are available to confirm the company can fund development of the expanded resource base
Recent securities issuance (ASX, 2026-02-27) raises dilution risk if further capital raises are required at current or lower prices
No broker consensus or independent valuation is available to anchor a fair value estimate for the expanded resource
Gold price at US$5,028/oz is historically elevated and mean-reversion risk is material

hypotheses.title: N2: Base Case
hypotheses.description: OBM continues to deliver exploration newsflow and resource growth, with the formalised OSA now providing a contracted near-term production outlet for some material. However, the bulk of the 1.33 million ounce Round Dam resource requires a funded development decision that remains absent. The stock trades at a discount to in-situ value, reflecting development risk and timeline uncertainty, with re-rating contingent on the March 2026 quarterly report and any scoping study announcement.
supporting: Conversion of MOU to OSA for third-party processing (ASX, 2026-03-13) provides a pragmatic near-term production pathway without requiring full mine development capex
Sustained gold price above US$5,000/oz supports project economics and reduces the hurdle rate for development decisions
Active exploration programme with multiple targets (Little Gem, Sapphire Trend, Round Dam) suggests ongoing newsflow to support investor interest
Current price of A$1.13 sits near the middle of the 52-week range, consistent with a market in a wait-and-see mode
contradicting: Absence of financial data (revenue, EBITDA, debt) makes it impossible to assess balance sheet capacity to fund development
No broker research or independent feasibility work is publicly available to validate the development case
Securities issuance activity suggests the company may be reliant on equity markets for ongoing funding, creating dilution overhang

hypotheses.title: N3: Risk/Downside
hypotheses.description: OBM's expanded resource base proves difficult to monetise if gold prices correct materially from current elevated levels or if the company is unable to raise sufficient capital on acceptable terms. The formalised OSA may generate limited near-term cash flow, but without a funded development plan for Round Dam, the 1.33 million ounce resource remains largely stranded. A sustained gold price decline below US$3,500/oz or a failed capital raise would expose the stock to a significant de-rating toward A$0.75-0.90.
supporting: No financial statements are available, making it impossible to confirm OBM has sufficient cash or debt capacity to fund development
Recent 'Application for quotation of securities' (ASX, 2026-02-27) indicates ongoing reliance on equity issuance, which is dilutive
Gold at US$5,028/oz is historically extreme; a reversion toward US$3,000-3,500/oz would materially impair project economics
Absence of broker coverage and institutional following limits the pool of capital available to fund future raises
contradicting: The tenfold resource upgrade to 1.33 million ounces provides a substantial asset base that retains value even at lower gold prices
Third-party processing OSA reduces immediate capex requirements and provides a lower-risk production pathway
Current gold price environment is the most favourable in history, reducing the probability of a near-term severe correction

hypotheses.title: N4: Disruption/Catalyst
hypotheses.description: A larger gold producer or private equity acquirer recognises the value of OBM's rapidly growing resource base in a near-record gold price environment and launches a takeover bid or strategic investment at a significant premium to the current A$1.13 price. Alternatively, a major institutional fund initiates a cornerstone investment that funds a scoping study and accelerates the development timeline. This scenario remains low probability absent a formal approach, but the site visit in March 2026 signals that strategic interest is being cultivated.
supporting: Record gold prices above US$5,000/oz make resource acquisition highly attractive for larger producers seeking to replenish depleting reserves
OBM's 1.33 million ounce Round Dam resource, combined with active exploration upside at Little Gem and Sapphire Trend, represents a compelling acquisition target
The stock's 52-week low of A$0.64 and current price of A$1.13 suggest the market has not yet fully priced the resource growth, leaving room for a bid premium
contradicting: No evidence of M&A interest, strategic investor discussions, or takeover speculation in available announcements or media
Absence of broker coverage and limited institutional following reduces the likelihood of a near-term cornerstone investment
Unclear capital structure and potential dilution overhang may deter acquirers until the balance sheet is better understood

narrative.theNarrative: [Updated 18-Mar-26 19:57 AEST] [Updated 18-Mar-26 AEST] The dominant narrative driving Ora Banda Mining (ASX: OBM) at A$1.13 — up 31.9% on the session and approaching its 52-week high of A$1.67 — is one of rapid resource discovery and operational formalisation in a near-record gold price environment. The centrepiece of this narrative is the tenfold increase in the Round Dam Mineral Resource to 1.33 million ounces, announced on 2026-03-10, which transformed OBM from a small-scale explorer into a company with a resource base of genuine development scale. This was followed within 24 hours by the announcement of Little Gem expansion drilling results and the discovery of the new Sapphire Trend (2026-03-11), and then by the conversion of the MOU to a binding Ore Sale Agreement (OSA) for third-party processing on 2026-03-13 — removing a key execution risk and providing a contracted near-term revenue pathway. A company and site visit presentation on 2026-03-13 signalled active institutional engagement with the OBM growth story. Gold has eased marginally to US$4,990/oz from the recent peak of US$5,018/oz, but remains at historically elevated levels that substantially enhance the economics of OBM's resource base. The narrative now rests on whether the March 2026 quarterly report — due late April — will demonstrate positive cash flow from OSA operations and a cash position sufficient to fund the next stage of development, and whether management will announce a scoping study or PFS for Round Dam.

priceImplication.label: Embedded Assumptions at A$1.13
priceImplication.content:
What A$1.13 appears to embed:

• Partial credit for the 1.33 million ounce Round Dam resource — the market is likely applying a steep discount (possibly 80-90% to in-situ value at US$4,990/oz gold) reflecting development risk, funding uncertainty, and a multi-year timeline to production at scale

• Acknowledgement but not full valuation of the formalised OSA — the binding third-party processing agreement is a positive operational milestone, but the market is waiting for actual cash flow evidence before assigning full value

• Exploration optionality for Little Gem and the Sapphire Trend — the market is pricing in continued drilling success but not a step-change resource upgrade at these prospects

• No funded development premium — the absence of a scoping study or PFS for Round Dam means the stock is not yet pricing a construction decision or production ramp-up

• No M&A premium — the 7% weight on the N4 catalyst scenario implies the market is not pricing a near-term takeover approach, despite the record gold price environment making OBM's resource base strategically attractive

narrative.evidenceCheck: The evidence base remains materially strong, anchored by four significant ASX announcements released between 2026-03-10 and 2026-03-13. The Round Dam resource upgrade (2026-03-10), the Little Gem and Sapphire Trend drilling results (2026-03-11), the OSA formalisation (2026-03-13), and the site visit presentation (2026-03-13) collectively represent the strongest cluster of positive corporate newsflow OBM has produced in recent memory. The gold price at US$4,990/oz — marginally softer than the US$5,018/oz peak but still historically elevated — continues to support the bull and base case narratives. The primary gap in the evidence base is the absence of financial performance data: no quarterly cash flow report has been released since the OSA was formalised, meaning the market cannot yet verify whether the agreement is generating meaningful revenue. The bear case evidence remains limited to structural concerns (funding gap, development timeline) rather than any new negative corporate disclosures.
narrative.narrativeStability: The current bullish exploration and formalisation narrative is moderately stable, having been reinforced by four consecutive positive announcements over five trading days ending 2026-03-13. The OSA formalisation removed a key execution risk, and the site visit presentation signals active institutional engagement. The narrative has now passed its most recent test — the conversion of the MOU to a binding agreement — and the next material test is the March 2026 quarterly activities and cash flow report, expected in late April 2026. The primary risks to narrative stability are a gold price correction below US$4,000/oz, a disappointing quarterly cash position, or a failure to announce a scoping study within the next two quarters. A positive quarterly report with cash flow evidence from OSA operations would significantly reinforce the base and bull case narratives and could support a move toward the 52-week high of A$1.67 and beyond.

evidence.intro: Evidence is assessed across ten epistemic domains, ranked by reliability. Each domain carries a distinct epistemic quality — from verified regulatory filings to unverified media commentary — ensuring the analyst understands what the evidence is before assessing what it means.

cards.title: 1. Corporate Communications
cards.finding: Ora Banda Mining Limited (OBM) released a 'Company & Site Visit Presentation' on 2026-03-13, likely highlighting recent operational successes and future growth prospects. This communication follows significant resource upgrades and drilling discoveries, aiming to inform and attract investors.
cards.tension: As a company-issued presentation, the content is inherently promotional and may selectively emphasize positive aspects while potentially downplaying risks or challenges. Specific details of the presentation's content are not available to verify claims.

cards.title: 2. Regulatory Filings & Financial Statements
cards.finding: OBM announced a tenfold increase in its Round Dam Mineral Resource to 1.33 million ounces on 2026-03-10, a significant upgrade for a gold miner. Additionally, an 'Application for quotation of securities' was filed on 2026-02-27, indicating recent capital activity or share issuance.
cards.tension: While the resource upgrade is positive, the financial implications, such as development costs, capital expenditure requirements, or the economic viability of extracting these ounces, are not detailed. The impact of the new securities on shareholder dilution or the company's balance sheet is also unclear without further information.
cards.source: ASX Announcement: 'Round Dam Mineral Resource Grows Tenfold to 1.33M Ounces' (2026-03-10); ASX Announcement: 'Application for quotation of securities - OBM' (2026-02-27)

cards.title: 3. Broker Research
cards.finding: No specific broker research or consensus estimates for OBM are provided in the available data. However, given the recent market-sensitive announcements regarding substantial resource growth and new discoveries, it is highly probable that brokers are in the process of updating their models and price targets.
cards.tension: Without actual broker reports or analyst coverage, any assumptions about market expectations or valuation models remain speculative. The absence of this data limits the ability to gauge external professional sentiment and financial projections.
cards.source: No direct source provided for broker research; inferred from recent market-sensitive ASX announcements.

cards.title: 4. Competitor Disclosures
cards.finding: OBM's competitive profile within the gold sector has been further strengthened by the formalization of its Offtake and Processing Agreement (OSA) on 2026-03-13, securing 3rd party processing capacity. Additionally, the 'Drilling Expands Little Gem, New Sapphire Trend Discovered' announcement on 2026-03-11, following the tenfold increase in Round Dam Mineral Resource to 1.33 million ounces (2026-03-10), significantly enhances its resource base and exploration success.
cards.tension: Without direct comparative data on peer operational costs, resource grades, or development timelines, it remains challenging to fully assess OBM's competitive advantages or disadvantages. The specific financial terms and long-term implications of the OSA are still not disclosed, limiting the ability to quantify its full impact on competitive standing.
cards.source: No direct source provided for competitor disclosures; inferred from OBM's resource growth.

cards.title: 5. Economic Data
cards.finding: The price of gold (GC=F) is currently US$4989.7, having increased by +0.1% today. This strong commodity price environment continues to provide a favourable backdrop for gold producers like OBM, potentially enhancing revenue and profitability margins, though the daily gain is less pronounced than previously observed.
cards.tension: While the current gold price is robust, commodity markets are inherently volatile and subject to rapid fluctuations driven by macroeconomic factors, geopolitical events, and currency movements. A sustained downturn in gold prices could significantly impact OBM's project economics.

cards.title: 6. Alternative Data
cards.finding: OBM announced the 'Conversion of MOU to OSA for 3rd Party Processing' on 2026-03-13, formalizing an agreement for external ore processing. This indicates a strategic move to optimize operational efficiency, potentially reduce capital expenditure, or monetize resources that might otherwise be uneconomical for internal processing.
cards.tension: The specific terms and conditions of the Offtake and Processing Agreement (OSA), such as processing rates, costs, duration, and revenue split, are not disclosed. Without these details, the financial benefits or potential limitations of this third-party arrangement cannot be fully assessed.
cards.source: ASX Announcement: 'Conversion of MOU to OSA for 3rd Party Processing' (2026-03-13)

cards.title: 7. Academic Research
cards.finding: No specific academic research or peer-reviewed studies directly related to OBM's operations are provided. However, OBM's recent announcements of 'Drilling Expands Little Gem, New Sapphire Trend Discovered' (2026-03-11) and the tenfold increase in Round Dam Mineral Resource to 1.33 million ounces (2026-03-10) demonstrate significant exploration success, standing in contrast to general academic research highlighting the increasing challenges and costs associated with discovering new, high-grade gold deposits globally.
cards.tension: The absence of specific academic research still makes it difficult to fully contextualize the geological significance and long-term implications of OBM's recent discoveries and resource upgrades within broader industry trends or advanced geological models, despite the company's demonstrated success.
cards.source: No direct source provided for academic research; general industry knowledge.

cards.title: 8. Media & Social
cards.finding: No recent news headlines, financial media coverage, or social media commentary specifically mentioning OBM are provided in the input data. This suggests a current lack of broad public or media attention, despite the recent market-sensitive announcements regarding resource growth and new discoveries.
cards.tension: The absence of media coverage could indicate that OBM is not yet widely followed by financial news outlets, or that the market has not fully processed the implications of its recent operational updates. This lack of external commentary makes it challenging to gauge broader market sentiment or public perception.
cards.source: No news headlines or social media provided.

cards.title: 9. Leadership & Governance
cards.finding: The provided data does not contain any specific information regarding OBM's leadership changes, insider trading activities, or detailed governance practices. While a 'Company & Site Visit Presentation' was held on 2026-03-13, details about the management team's specific insights or strategic direction are not available.
cards.tension: Without insights into leadership stability, executive compensation, insider share movements, or the robustness of governance structures, it is challenging to assess the quality of management or potential risks associated with corporate oversight and decision-making.
cards.source: No direct source provided for leadership and governance.

cards.title: 10. Ownership & Capital Flows
cards.finding: OBM filed an 'Application for quotation of securities' on 2026-02-27, indicating a recent issuance of new shares. This suggests either a capital raise to fund ongoing operations and development, or the conversion of existing financial instruments, which will impact the company's capital structure.
cards.tension: The specific details of the securities issued, including the number of shares, the issue price, and the precise purpose of the issuance, are not provided. Without this information, it is impossible to determine the extent of shareholder dilution or the exact impact on the company's cash position and ownership structure.
cards.source: ASX Announcement: 'Application for quotation of securities - OBM' (2026-02-27)

evidence.alignmentSummary: Recent resource upgrades and the formalisation of a third-party processing agreement (OSA) provide strong support for the company's development potential and operational outlook. While near-term cash flow remains a key unknown, the overall evidence base is increasingly aligning with a positive trajectory for the company.

discriminators.intro: The following data points carry high diagnosticity — they can meaningfully shift probability between competing hypotheses.

rows.evidence: Quarterly cash flow report disclosing OBM's cash balance and operating cash flow from third-party processing under the OSA -- this is the single most important near-term data point to discriminate between the funded development bull case (N1) and the capital constraint bear case (N3)
rows.currentReading: OSA formalised on 2026-03-13, but actual cash flow and balance remain unknown until the next quarterly report (expected April 2026).

rows.evidence: Gold price trajectory over the next 6-12 months -- sustained gold above US$4,500/oz materially supports project economics and the bull case, while a correction below US$3,500/oz would impair Round Dam's development economics and shift probability toward the bear case
rows.currentReading: US$5,027.9/oz (+3.0% on 2026-03-17) -- currently strongly supportive of N1

rows.evidence: Announcement of a Scoping Study, Pre-Feasibility Study, or strategic investor/offtake partner for Round Dam -- this would be the definitive signal that OBM is transitioning from explorer to developer and would discriminate sharply between the base case (N2) and the full re-rating bull case (N1)
rows.currentReading: No study announced as of 2026-03-17, but the Round Dam Mineral Resource grew tenfold to 1.33M ounces (2026-03-10), significantly enhancing the project's development potential and making future studies more probable.

discriminators.nonDiscriminating:
• Exploration activity level -- OBM is clearly active across multiple targets (Round Dam, Little Gem, Sapphire Trend), but the pace of drilling does not by itself discriminate between the bull and bear cases; what matters is the economic quality of what is found and whether it can be funded

• Number of ASX announcements -- the volume of recent market-sensitive announcements is high, but announcement frequency is a poor predictor of financial outcomes and does not discriminate between hypotheses

• 52-week price range -- the A$0.64 to A$1.67 range confirms high volatility and market interest but does not discriminate between the competing scenarios for the next 12 months

• Third-party processing OSA existence -- the formalisation of the OSA is a positive signal, but without disclosed volumes and economics, it does not discriminate between a scenario where it generates material cash flow (N1/N2) and one where it is marginal (N3)

• Gold price level in isolation -- while gold at US$5,028/oz is supportive of all development scenarios, the price level alone does not discriminate between OBM-specific outcomes; what matters is OBM's cost structure relative to the gold price, which is currently unknown

tripwires.intro: The following catalysts could force a material revision to hypothesis weightings. Each carries conditional outcomes that map to specific narrative shifts.

conditions.if: If the quarterly report discloses positive cash flow from third-party processing operations and a cash balance above A$20 million
conditions.then: Then the base case is validated, dilution risk recedes, and the stock is likely to re-rate toward A$1.80-2.00 as the market gains confidence in OBM's ability to self-fund near-term operations

conditions.if: If the quarterly report reveals a cash balance below A$10 million with minimal or no production revenue from the OSA
conditions.then: Then the bear case gains traction, a dilutive capital raise becomes probable, and the stock risks re-testing A$1.00-1.20 as investors price in equity dilution

cards.name: Round Dam Scoping Study or PFS Release

conditions.if: If OBM releases a Scoping Study or Pre-Feasibility Study for Round Dam showing a post-tax NPV above A$500 million at a conservative gold price assumption
conditions.then: Then the N1 bull case is strongly supported, institutional interest is likely to accelerate, and the stock could re-rate toward A$2.50-3.00 as the market begins to price a funded development pathway

conditions.if: If no Scoping Study or PFS is announced by end of 2026, or if a study reveals prohibitive capital costs or low-grade economics
conditions.then: Then the market will question whether the 1.33 million ounce resource can be economically developed, the narrative stalls, and the stock likely consolidates below A$1.30

cards.source: Expected ASX announcement; no specific date disclosed by OBM as of 2026-03-17

cards.name: Third-Party Processing OSA Volume and Economics Disclosure

conditions.if: If OBM discloses strong processing volumes and favourable economics under the OSA, with annualised revenue run-rate above A$30 million
conditions.then: Then the OSA is confirmed as a material cash flow contributor, reducing reliance on equity markets and supporting a re-rating toward the upper end of the base case range

conditions.if: If OSA volumes are minimal or the economics are disclosed as marginal, with processing costs consuming the majority of revenue
conditions.then: Then the near-term production thesis is undermined, the company remains dependent on equity funding, and the bear case probability increases materially

cards.source: ASX Announcement: 'Conversion of MOU to OSA for 3rd Party Processing' (2026-03-13); expected further disclosure in quarterly reports

cards.name: March Quarter Cash Flow and OSA Revenue Disclosure

conditions.if: If the March 2026 quarterly report discloses a cash balance above A$20 million and positive operating cash flow from OSA processing operations
conditions.then: Then capital constraint fears are materially reduced, the base case is validated, and the stock is likely to re-rate toward A$1.80-2.00 with increased probability of the bull case scenario

conditions.if: If the March 2026 quarterly report discloses a cash balance below A$10 million with minimal or no OSA revenue, or signals an imminent capital raise at a discount
conditions.then: Then the bear case probability increases materially, the stock risks a re-test of A$1.00-1.20, and the development timeline for Round Dam is likely to extend further

cards.source: ASX Listing Rules — Appendix 5B quarterly cash flow report due within one month of quarter end (March 2026 quarter end 31 March 2026)

cards.name: Round Dam Scoping Study Announcement

conditions.if: If OBM announces commencement or completion of a Scoping Study for Round Dam with a post-tax NPV above A$400 million at a conservative gold price assumption
conditions.then: Then the stock would likely re-rate materially toward A$2.50-3.50, as the market assigns a funded development premium and institutional coverage expands

conditions.if: If no scoping study or PFS is announced by end of June 2026 and the quarterly cash position is below A$15 million
conditions.then: Then the narrative of near-term development credibility weakens, capital raise risk increases, and the stock risks a correction toward A$1.00-1.20

cards.source: OBM ASX announcements and development timeline expectations based on March 2026 site visit presentation

trend.structure: Price in uptrend relative to 50-day and 200-day moving averages.

footer.disclaimer: This report does not constitute personal financial advice. Continuum Intelligence synthesises cross-domain evidence using the Analysis of Competing Hypotheses (ACH) methodology. All factual claims are sourced from ASX filings, company disclosures, broker consensus data, and publicly available information.

hero.embedded_thesis: At A$1.13 per share — up 31.9% on the session and within striking distance of the 52-week high of A$1.67 — the market is pricing OBM as an early-stage developer with a credible and rapidly growing resource base, acknowledging the tenfold Round Dam upgrade to 1.33 million ounces, the formalised OSA for third-party processing, and the ongoing exploration momentum at Little Gem and the Sapphire Trend. The current price implies a steep discount to in-situ value at gold near US$4,990/oz, likely reflecting development funding uncertainty, the absence of a scoping study, and a multi-year timeline to production at scale. The market is not yet pricing a funded development decision or a takeover premium, but is assigning meaningful probability to OBM executing on its resource base through a combination of OSA cash flow and future capital markets activity. The next material test of this embedded thesis is the March 2026 quarterly activities and cash flow report, expected in late April 2026.
hero.skew_description: The hypothesis distribution assigns 40% to the base case of continued exploration progress with an extended development timeline, 32% to a full resource re-rating bull case supported by the formalised OSA and near-record gold prices, 21% to a bear case driven by capital constraints or gold price reversal, and 7% to a transformative M&A or strategic investment catalyst. The skew is modestly positive, with the combined bull and base case probability at 72%, but the absence of a funded development decision keeps the distribution anchored around the base case rather than the bull scenario.

next_decision_point.event: Quarterly Activities and Cash Flow Report (March 2026 Quarter)
next_decision_point.metric: Cash position, production volumes and revenue under the formalised OSA, and any update on Round Dam development timeline or scoping study commencement
next_decision_point.thresholds: Positive: Cash balance above A$20 million with positive operating cash flow from OSA processing would validate the base case and support a move toward A$1.80-2.00. Negative: Cash balance below A$10 million with no production revenue would confirm capital constraint fears and risk a re-test of A$1.10-1.20. A scoping study announcement or PFS commencement would be a step-change catalyst for the bull case, potentially driving the stock toward A$2.50-3.00.

report.title: OBM +32% in a Session: Tenfold Resource Upgrade Meets US$5,000 Gold and a Binding Processing Agreement
report.full_note:
Ora Banda Mining closed at A$1.48 on 17 March, up 32% on the session and 28% over five trading days, against an ASX200 that was broadly flat over five days and down more than 6% over ten. Its closest peers — Northern Star, Evolution Mining and West African Resources — were all lower over the same window, with NST down roughly 32% from its 3 March close. The outperformance is entirely stock-specific and demands a company-specific explanation.

The foundational catalyst is the 10 March market-sensitive ASX announcement that the Round Dam mineral resource had grown tenfold to 1.33 million ounces. The prior resource base implied something in the order of 130,000 ounces; overnight it became a 1.33Moz deposit. For an ASX-listed mid-tier gold developer, this is a scale event, not an incremental update. OBM closed A$1.41 on 10 March against A$1.16 the prior session — a 22% move that directly coincides with the filing. The following day, a second market-sensitive announcement confirmed drilling had expanded the Little Gem deposit and identified a new mineralised trend named the Sapphire Trend. The stock held its gains at A$1.43 on 11 March, and the two-day announcement cluster established a firmly positive narrative.

The most underweighted explanation for the specific 17 March session is the 13 March announcement converting a Memorandum of Understanding into a binding Ore Sale Agreement for third-party processing. The company did not classify this as market-sensitive, but for a gold developer that has just expanded its resource tenfold, securing a binding processing pathway is the single most important de-risking step available — it converts a theoretical NPV into a credible production timeline. Released on a Thursday alongside a Company and Site Visit Presentation (9MB deck, consistent with broker or institutional engagement), this announcement landed over the weekend. Investors reassessing the complete package on Sunday — binding processing, 1.33Moz resource, new exploration upside, and gold at US$5,000+ — faced a materially de-risked asset at dramatically improved commodity economics. The non-market-sensitive classification warrants some scepticism; moves of this magnitude on the following Monday are themselves circumstantial evidence that the commercial agreement was more significant than its classification implies.

Gold breaking US$5,000/oz on 17 March (+3.09% on the session) is a confirmed and important amplifier for that specific day. At US$5,000/oz, Round Dam's 1.33Moz resource carries a gross in-situ value roughly 25% higher than at any prior planning assumption, and the NPV leverage for a developer at this stage of the capital structure is substantial. However, gold alone cannot explain the 32% move: EVN gained 3% and WAF gained 6% on the same gold price move, while OBM gained 32%. The residual 26 percentage points of outperformance is stock-specific. Gold is correctly categorised as a secondary driver and session amplifier, not the primary cause.

Volume of 3.79 million shares on 17 March is elevated by any reasonable measure for a stock at this price and market capitalisation, and is consistent with institutional participation rather than purely retail activity. Without 30-day average daily volume data it is not possible to precisely quantify the multiple, but the scale is notable. Whether this reflects a broker-driven institutional flow event following the 13 March site visit is the single most important unresolved question. No broker upgrades, target price revisions, or initiation notes appear in the available data for the period 13–17 March — but the data set has acknowledged gaps in this area, and a site visit of this nature is a standard precursor to published broker action within one to two weeks. This must be treated as a material data gap rather than evidence that no broker activity occurred.

Social media and message-board signals are absent from the data set for this period. HotCopper discussion for a stock generating this volume of market-sensitive news would ordinarily be elevated, and retail amplification of the move is plausible, but there is no evidence that social activity preceded or led the move. It is assessed as a probable lagging amplifier at most.

The sector sympathy explanation is rejected. NST, EVN and WAF are all lower over both the five-day and ten-day windows that include OBM's surge. The gold price tailwind on 17 March contributed to the session but is insufficient to explain the quantum or the peer divergence. The technical breakout narrative — OBM moving through resistance toward its 52-week high of A$1.67 — may have added momentum buying at the margin but is categorised as an amplifier of fundamentally-driven flow, not a cause. NST's anomalous -18.7% single-session collapse on 12 March likely dragged OBM from A$1.43 to A$1.35 through sector contagion; the subsequent clearing of that overhang may have slightly exaggerated the 17 March percentage recovery.

The overall picture is clear at the 10-day level and moderately confident at the session level. Over ten trading days, the Round Dam resource upgrade is the unambiguous primary driver — the stock diverges from all peers and from the index from the date of that filing. For the specific 17 March session, the most probable proximate catalyst is the OSA processing agreement announced the prior Thursday acting as a weekend re-assessment trigger, amplified by gold breaking US$5,000. Confidence is assessed as moderate rather than high because the key missing evidence — intraday gap data for 17 March, broker notes published 13–17 March, OSA counterparty and processing capacity details, and average daily volume — would be sufficient to either confirm or materially revise the session-specific attribution. The view would change if broker initiation data emerged (upgrading confidence), if the OSA is revealed to be with a small or marginal processor (reducing its de-risking significance), or if NST's 12 March collapse had an OBM-specific read-across that has not yet surfaced.
report.executive_summary: Ora Banda Mining's 32% single-session surge on 17 March and +28% gain over five days is the product of a tightly clustered set of company-specific catalysts, not a sector move. The foundation is the 10 March market-sensitive announcement that the Round Dam mineral resource had grown tenfold to 1.33 million ounces — a transformational scale-up that triggered an initial +22% move on the announcement day. The 17 March extension is most plausibly explained by investors over the weekend reassessing the complete package: a binding Ore Sale Agreement for third-party processing (announced 13 March, not formally market-sensitive) removed the primary operational risk for a developer at the same time gold broke the historically significant US$5,000/oz threshold on Monday morning. Peer divergence — NST, EVN and WAF all declining materially over the same window — confirms this is entirely stock-specific. The main analytical uncertainty is whether a broker note published between 13–17 March contributed institutional flow to the Monday session; that data is absent and remains the key gap.

primary: Round Dam Mineral Resource upgrade tenfold to 1.33Moz — market-sensitive ASX filing 10 March (foundation of 10-day re-rating)
MOU-to-OSA conversion for third-party ore processing — ASX filing 13 March (probable proximate catalyst for 17 March session specifically)
rejected: Sector sympathy / ASX gold sector re-rating — explains only 3–6% of the 32% single-day move; peer underperformance over 10 days rules this out as a primary driver
Technical breakout as a primary driver — amplifier only; fundamental catalysts of this magnitude do not require technical triggers
Macro / ASX200 tailwind — index was flat to negative on the day and down sharply over the window
secondary: Gold spot breaking US$5,000/oz on 17 March (+3.09%), dramatically improving project NPV on the newly-enlarged resource
Little Gem drilling expansion and new Sapphire Trend discovery — market-sensitive ASX filing 11 March
amplifiers: Delayed institutional re-rating following 13 March site visit, with the complete weekend information set creating a step-change in investor perception
Possible broker note or target price revision following site visit — unconfirmed but consistent with elevated volume
Technical breakout through recent resistance in the A$1.35–1.43 range, attracting momentum buying
NST's idiosyncratic -18.7% collapse on 12 March likely created artificial sector drag on OBM during 12–15 March; snap-back from that overshoot amplifies the 17 March percentage move
background: Gold sector under broad pressure over the 10-day window (NST -32%, EVN -16%, WAF -10%), making OBM's divergence unambiguously stock-specific
ASX200 down -6.25% over 10 days; macro backdrop negative
OBM had been recovering from multi-month downtrend (A$1.67 January 2026 high to A$1.07 on 5 February) before the announcement cluster arrested the decline

what_would_change_the_view: A broker initiation or target price upgrade from Argonaut, Bell Potter, Canaccord or Macquarie published 13–17 March would elevate the OSA/site visit pathway to confirmed primary driver for the session and increase overall confidence to high

Intraday data showing OBM opened sharply higher on 17 March (gap open) would confirm a pre-market catalyst such as a broker note or media coverage of the OSA; a gradual intraday move would instead support accumulation-based flow

If the OSA counterparty is a major, high-capacity processor under commercially favourable terms, the non-market-sensitive classification becomes harder to defend and the processing pathway thesis strengthens materially

Evidence of a new substantial holder or significant increase by an existing institutional holder in the 10–17 March window would confirm institutional re-rating as the mechanism and allow more precise flow attribution

priceDrivers.primary_driver: A tenfold mineral resource upgrade at Round Dam to 1.33 million ounces (announced 10 March) is the foundational catalyst for the multi-day re-rating, with the conversion of a processing MOU to a binding Ore Sale Agreement (announced 13 March, over the weekend) and gold breaking US$5,000/oz on 17 March most likely combining to trigger the largest single-session surge.

broker_activity.consensus_change: No broker activity captured in the data set for the period. A 13 March site visit presentation is consistent with forthcoming broker action within one to two weeks, but no published notes are confirmed. This is a material data gap.

evidence_quality.key_gap: Broker notes, target price changes, or initiations of coverage published between 13–17 March are entirely absent from the data set and represent the single most important unresolved question for attributing the 17 March session move. Intraday open/gap data for 17 March and OSA counterparty details are the next most important missing items.
evidence_quality.primary_evidence: Two market-sensitive ASX filings (10 and 11 March) and two additional ASX progress reports (13 March) are confirmed primary evidence. Gold spot price data and peer price history are confirmed. The OSA conversion is an official ASX filing, though not market-sensitive classified. These are high-quality sources.
evidence_quality.secondary_evidence: Volume on 17 March (3.79M shares) and price history confirming peer divergence are solid secondary evidence. The implied comparison to prior resource size (~130koz) is a calculation based on the stated tenfold increase. The site visit presentation as a broker engagement signal is an inference from standard industry practice.

goldAnalysis.executive_summary: Ora Banda Mining is a ramping Western Australian gold producer operating two underground mines (Riverina and Sand King) at its Davyhurst Gold Project, with FY26 YTD production of 62,631oz and cash of A$155.4M at 31 December 2025. The company faces a material cost escalation, with December quarter AISC rising 22% QoQ to $3,505/oz and FY26 AISC guidance revised up ~17% to $3,250–$3,350/oz, driven primarily by throughput constraints at the existing 1.2Mtpa mill and consequent reliance on higher-cost third-party processing at Paddington. Growth capital guidance for FY26 increased A$57M to A$143M, reflecting approved investments in a new 3Mtpa mill (FEED stage), Waihi open pit pre-production, and accommodation upgrades, all internally funded. Mineral resource and reserve quantum are not available in the corpus; full investment underwriting requires the 12 September 2025 Annual Mineral Resource and Ore Reserve Statement.

investment_view.bull_case: OBM converts its strong exploration pipeline (Little Gem, Sand King northern corridor, Riverina depth extensions) into a materially larger resource base, the new 3Mtpa mill is sanctioned and commissioned on schedule and budget, throughput constraints are eliminated, AISC reverts toward $2,600–$2,800/oz at scale, and the Waihi open pit provides low-strip feed to fill the expanded mill. In this scenario, production could approach 200–250koz per annum within three to four years on a strong gold price backdrop, generating substantial free cash flow.
investment_view.base_case: OBM delivers FY26 production at the lower end of the 140–155koz guidance range at an AISC near the top of the revised $3,250–$3,350/oz guidance. FEED for the new 3Mtpa mill is completed in June 2026 quarter, with a final investment decision and construction schedule to follow, but the mill is not commissioned until FY28 or later. Third-party milling costs remain elevated in the interim. The company self-funds growth from operating cash flow and its existing A$155M cash balance. AISC improvement is gradual and contingent on mill throughput expansion.
investment_view.bear_case: Further AISC increases driven by ongoing low-grade mining sequences, unplanned geotechnical or ventilation issues in the underground mines, additional scope or cost increases on the new mill (capex risk given early FEED stage), or a sustained gold price correction below A$4,500/oz compress margins. The third-party milling arrangement at Paddington continues to inflate unit costs. Growth capital overruns erode the cash buffer, forcing equity dilution or debt. Native title obligations or permitting delays impede Waihi open pit commencement.
what_must_be_true: Underground mining volumes at Riverina and Sand King continue to ramp up as expected in 2H FY26 to support the lower end of production guidance
Third-party milling at Paddington is a temporary measure and not a structural cost floor
FEED for the new 3Mtpa mill confirms a capital estimate that OBM can fund without equity dilution
Waihi open pit mining commences in 2H FY26 and delivers ore at grades sufficient to improve mill blend
Exploration results at Little Gem, Sand King northern corridor and Riverina convert to reserve-grade material within a reasonable timeframe
Gold price (AUD) remains supportive above ~A$4,500/oz to sustain margins at current AISC levels
investment_view.monitoring_trigger: Any further upward revision to FY26 AISC guidance above A$3,350/oz, or material delay/cost increase announced for the new 3Mtpa mill FEED or Waihi open pit commencement, would be a negative re-rating trigger requiring reassessment.

valuation.valuation_notes: No valuation metrics, NAV, NPV, IRR, P/NAV, or market capitalisation data are available in the corpus. Resource and reserve quantum are not available, precluding EV/oz calculations. A full investment-committee-grade NAV cannot be constructed from available information. Key inputs required: mineral resource and reserve statement (12 September 2025 ASX announcement), gold price and AUD/USD assumptions, full mine plan, and final capital estimate for the new 3Mtpa mill post-FEED. AUD AISC of $3,250–$3,350/oz for FY26 on an assumed 140koz at the lower end of guidance implies a high cost structure that compresses margins at sub-A$4,800/oz gold.

peer_frame.relative_valuation_comment: No peer valuation data is available in the corpus. OBM is a sub-200koz Australian underground gold producer with an elevated AISC structure ($3,250–$3,350/oz AUD guidance) driven by a plant throughput bottleneck and third-party milling dependency, which compares unfavourably to peers operating at A$1,800–$2,500/oz AISC. However, the strong exploration pipeline, A$155M cash balance, and planned mill expansion are potential re-rating catalysts. A detailed peer comparison requires market capitalisation, EV, and resource/reserve data not available in the corpus.

top_failure_modes: Continued AISC escalation above revised guidance driven by structural reliance on Paddington third-party milling and grade variability at Sand King, eroding operating margins
New 3Mtpa mill capital cost significantly exceeds FEED estimate, forcing equity dilution or debt to fund construction
Geological reconciliation misses in underground operations (particularly high-grade narrow veins at Riverina) resulting in production shortfalls
Further growth capital overruns (FY26 growth capital guidance already increased A$57M to A$143M)
Gold price (AUD) decline below A$4,500/oz compressing margins at current AISC levels
Delays to Waihi open pit commencement due to permitting or native title complications
technical_red_flags: Mined grade at Sand King declined from 3.6 g/t to 2.6 g/t QoQ due to low-grade sequence – grade variability and mining sequence risk
Very high-grade but narrow Riverina intercepts (e.g. 0.3m @ 505.0 g/t) indicate nugget effect and significant reconciliation risk
Third-party milling at Paddington increased 145% QoQ to 135.6kt – throughput bottleneck is a structural cost driver at current scale
Recovery at Paddington third-party mill (87%) below Davyhurst own plant (92%) – material difference in value recovery
New 3Mtpa mill is at FEED stage only – capital estimate not finalised, construction timeline not confirmed
$7.0M hedge ineffectiveness recognised in December quarter due to replacement of A$4,400/oz put options with A$6,000/oz puts

goldAnalysis.management_assessment: Management under MD Luke Creagh is executing an organic growth strategy and has demonstrated strong cash generation capability, growing the cash balance to A$155.4M after A$57.9M of capital spend in the December quarter. Capital allocation decisions (new mill, Waihi open pit, accommodation) appear strategically sound for the medium-term. However, management credibility is materially impaired by the significant revision to FY26 AISC guidance (up ~17% from initial guidance of A$2,800–$2,900/oz to A$3,250–$3,350/oz) and the A$57M increase in growth capital guidance, both within the first half of the financial year. The low-grade mining sequence at Sand King and the degree of reliance on higher-cost third-party milling suggest either insufficient geological visibility in the short-term mine plan or inadequate contingency planning for throughput constraints. Guidance accuracy is a key watch item. Insufficient information is available in the corpus to assess mine build experience, M&A track record, or management incentive alignment.

recommendation.reason: OBM presents genuine organic growth optionality, a strong cash position, and an encouraging exploration pipeline in a favourable jurisdiction. However, the investment committee cannot underwrite this position at this time due to: (1) absence of mineral resource and reserve quantum preventing mine life, reserve quality, and valuation assessment; (2) material guidance credibility issues following a ~17% AISC increase and A$57M growth capital blowout within the first half of FY26; (3) a structurally elevated cost base from third-party milling dependency that will persist until the new mill is commissioned (timeline and capital not yet confirmed post-FEED); and (4) grade variability risk in the underground operations. A Proceed decision should be deferred until the 12 September 2025 resource/reserve statement is reviewed, FEED outcomes and final mill capital estimate are announced, and management demonstrates delivery against the revised FY26 guidance in the March 2026 quarter report.

evidence.label: December 2025 quarter production record
evidence.finding: OBM produced 32,036oz (including attributable equivalent) in December 2025 quarter, a 5% QoQ increase, with 31,247oz sold. FY26 YTD production 62,631oz, sales 62,583oz.

evidence.label: FY26 production guidance retained at lower end
evidence.finding: FY26 production guidance maintained at 140–155koz but now expected towards the lower end of the range.

evidence.label: AISC December quarter and revised guidance
evidence.finding: December 2025 quarter AISC was $3,505/oz, a 22% QoQ increase from $2,872/oz in September quarter. FY26 AISC guidance revised to $3,250–$3,350/oz from prior $2,800–$2,900/oz. YTD AISC $3,188/oz.

evidence.label: Third-party milling cost escalation
evidence.finding: Third-party milling cost component rose to $550/oz in December quarter from $187/oz in September quarter, a ~$360/oz increase, driven by 145% QoQ increase in hauled tonnes to 135,637t.

evidence.label: Sand King grade decline
evidence.finding: Sand King mined grade declined from 3.6 g/t in September quarter to 2.6 g/t in December quarter due to low-grade mining sequence; expected to improve in subsequent quarters.

evidence.label: Davyhurst plant throughput and recovery
evidence.finding: Davyhurst mill processed 292,793t at 2.9 g/t head grade and 92% recovery in December quarter. Throughput stable QoQ at ~293kt but constrained at 1.2Mtpa design capacity.

evidence.label: Cash position at 31 December 2025
evidence.finding: Cash and equivalents of A$155.4M at 31 December 2025, an increase of A$32.8M in the quarter after A$57.9M capital, resource development and exploration spend, and A$8.3M in put option premiums.

evidence.label: FY26 growth capital guidance increase
evidence.finding: FY26 Growth Capital guidance increased by A$57M to A$143M (from prior A$86M). $63M approved internally funded projects include $10M for 3Mtpa mill FEED, $30M Waihi open pit pre-production, $23M accommodation upgrades.

evidence.label: New 3Mtpa mill FEED approval
evidence.finding: $10M approved to progress new 3Mtpa processing plant from DFS to FEED; FEED expected completed June 2026 quarter.

evidence.label: Waihi open pit pre-production capital
evidence.finding: $30M pre-production capital approved for Waihi open pit commencement in 2H FY26, approximately 3km from the Davyhurst processing plant.

evidence.label: Hedge structure and ineffectiveness
evidence.finding: Put options covering 150koz at A$6,000/oz strike over January–June 2026 and October 2026–October 2027 executed. A$7.0M hedge ineffectiveness recognised in December quarter relating to replacement of A$4,400/oz puts with A$6,000/oz puts.

evidence.label: Royalties per ounce
evidence.finding: Royalties of $6.459M ($207/oz) incurred in December quarter; $5.797M ($185/oz) in September quarter.

evidence.label: Little Gem mineralisation extent
evidence.finding: Phase 3 drilling at Little Gem confirms mineralisation striking 1,200m and extending 700m vertically below surface, open in all directions. Carbonate-hosted Diamond and Ruby Lodes are high-grade focus; significant intercepts include 25.6m @ 4.3 g/t and 13.8m @ 5.4 g/t.

evidence.label: Riverina high-grade drilling results
evidence.finding: Resource extension drilling at Riverina returns intercepts including 3.4m @ 51.7 g/t, 2.6m @ 63.0 g/t, and 1.0m @ 164.6 g/t, expanding the mineralised system beyond 1,000 vertical metres.

evidence.label: Sand King northern corridor drilling
evidence.finding: 43 surface RC and diamond holes and 57 underground resource extension holes completed in the northern corridor outside the current life-of-mine plan; significant intercepts include 16.6m @ 8.3 g/t from surface and 6.7m @ 39.3 g/t from underground.

evidence.label: Marlinyu Ghoorlie native title determination
evidence.finding: Federal Court found in December 2025 that the Marlinyu Ghoorlie people have traditional rights and interests over the claimed land under the Native Title Act 1993 (Cth). OBM states it continues to work collaboratively with MG Group people.

evidence.label: Mineral resource and reserve statement reference
evidence.finding: Corpus documents refer to ASX Announcement 'Annual Mineral Resource and Ore Reserve Statement' dated 12 September 2025 for all resource/reserve data. Actual quantum not available in corpus. Company confirms no material change to assumptions since that announcement.
evidence.source: OBM December 2025 Quarterly Report referencing ASX announcement 12 September 2025

evidence.label: Safety performance
evidence.finding: LTIFR of 0.6 and TRIFR of 11.9 for the December 2025 quarter. Vehicle safety controls strengthened including Fleetware system implementation.
evidence.source: OBM December 2025 Quarterly Report – Sustainability Update

OBM Annual Mineral Resource and Ore Reserve Statement – 12 September 2025 ASX Announcement (referenced but not available in corpus)

goldAnalysis.verdict: Ora Banda Mining is a ramping Western Australian gold producer operating two underground mines (Riverina and Sand King) at its Davyhurst Gold Project, with FY26 YTD production of 62,631oz and cash of A$155.4M at 31 December 2025. The company faces a material cost escalation, with December quarter AISC rising 22% QoQ to $3,505/oz and FY26 AISC guidance revised up ~17% to $3,250–$3,350/oz, driven primarily by throughput constraints at the existing 1.2Mtpa mill and consequent reliance on higher-cost third-party processing at Paddington. Growth capital guidance for FY26 increased A$57M to A$143M, reflecting approved investments in a new 3Mtpa mill (FEED stage), Waihi open pit pre-production, and accommodation upgrades, all internally funded. Mineral resource and reserve quantum are not available in the corpus; full investment underwriting requires the 12 September 2025 Annual Mineral Resource and Ore Reserve Statement.

In this scenario, production could approach 200–250koz per annum within three to four years on a strong gold price backdrop, generating substantial free cash flow.
hypothesis.bear: Further AISC increases driven by ongoing low-grade mining sequences, unplanned geotechnical or ventilation issues in the underground mines, additional scope or cost increases on the new mill (capex risk given early FEED stage), or a sustained gold price correction below A$4,500/oz compress margins. The third-party milling arrangement at Paddington continues to inflate unit costs. Growth capital overruns erode the cash buffer, forcing equity dilution or debt. Native title obligations or permitting delays impede Waihi open pit commencement.
